games.title: Heat: Tunnel Vision
games.opinion: This is the second expansion for Heat: Pedal to the Metal. It's rated 8.2 on BGA. It introduces new features like tunnels (where players cannot discard cards), a Front Wing upgrade card that allows for 'Boost' and 'Draft' mechanics (slipstreaming), and two new maps: España (Spain) which features tunnels, and Nederland (Netherlands) which is a quicker map without tunnels. It also adds four new event cards for the Championship System.

games.title: Heat: Pedal to the Metal
games.opinion: The base game for the Heat series, rated 8.0 on BGA. The reviewer states it's one of their favorite games to play on BGA and they constantly come back to it.

games.title: Heat: Heavy Rain
games.opinion: The first expansion for Heat: Pedal to the Metal, rated 8.1 on BGA. It introduces wet conditions and new maps.

summary: This video introduces and showcases the 'Heat: Tunnel Vision' expansion for 'Heat: Pedal to the Metal'. It highlights the new game mechanics such as tunnels, the Front Wing upgrade card which allows for slipstreaming (drafting), and the two new maps included in the expansion: España (Spain) and Nederland (Netherlands). The video also mentions the BGA ratings for the base game and its first expansion, 'Heat: Heavy Rain', and the reviewer expresses their appreciation for the game series.
classification: new game including how to play and rating
_vid_title: New to BGA: Heat — Tunnel Vision, How to Play on BGA

games.title: Love Letter
games.opinion: The reviewer expresses excitement for Love Letter returning to Board Game Arena (BGA) in beta with updated artwork and new cards (Spy, Chancellor). He calls it a 'great tiny little game' suitable for various casual settings and finds the BGA implementation very well done and convenient for learning card effects. He concludes it's a 'good game to be on BGA'.

summary: The video announces the return of the board game Love Letter to Board Game Arena (BGA) in beta, highlighting that it was previously removed due to publishing rights issues. The reviewer shows the game's updated artwork and new cards (Spy, Chancellor) available in this version. He provides a brief overview of the gameplay mechanics, explaining how to play and the objective of keeping the highest card or eliminating opponents. The reviewer gives positive quick impressions of the BGA implementation.
classification: how to play
_vid_title: It's Back, this Loved Game is Back to BGA in Beta.

summary: This video is a detailed tutorial on how to play the board game Watergate. It explains the asymmetric gameplay where one player controls Nixon and the other plays as the Editor. It covers the game's three phases: initial, card, and evaluation, detailing how players draw cards, use card actions and values, move momentum and initiative tokens on the research track, and manage evidence on the board to achieve their respective win conditions.
classification: how to play
_vid_title: Watergate How to Play on BGA Tutorial

games.title: Tacta
games.opinion: Tacta has simple choices and a luck element. The reviewer found it easier to play on BoardGameArena due to instant feedback on valid placements, but generally found the game frustrating and was not excited to play it, rating it a 5/10.

summary: This video provides an overview and review of the game Tacta, including its player count, playing time, and genre. It shows the game's BoardGameGeek statistics, explains the objective and core gameplay mechanics with visual examples, highlights important rules regarding card placement, and concludes with the reviewer's personal opinion on the game's turned-based suitability and overall worth.
classification: new game including how to play and rating
_vid_title: New to BGA: Tacta — Is It Worth Your Time?

games.title: Habitats
games.opinion: The game has a numeric rating of 7.4 from the community on BoardGameGeek, and the reviewer appears to be playing it for the first time, providing a tutorial on its mechanics and scoring.

summary: This video provides a comprehensive tutorial on how to play the board game Habitats. It covers the game's objective, turn structure, rules for taking and placing tiles from the market into a personal preserve, the concept of habitats, scoring conditions for various animal and flower tiles based on adjacent terrain types, and the scoring mechanics for special tiles like gates, watchtowers, and tourist tiles. The video also explains how end-of-year goals are scored and how points are tracked throughout the game's three years.
classification: how to play
_vid_title: How to Play Habitats on BGA.

games.title: Stem & Branch
games.opinion: A thinky card game, good for learning and practicing in co-op mode. The reviewer enjoys co-op games on BGA and this is a cool addition. The strategic elements of planning card plays and managing elements and animals are highlighted.

summary: This video provides a detailed explanation of the rules and gameplay of the card game Stem & Branch, available on BoardGameArena. It covers drawing and playing cards, summoning animals, scoring years (points) based on animal and element tokens, and explains mechanics like cost reduction and upgrading animals. The video concludes with a review and a personal rating of the game.
classification: new game including how to play and rating
_vid_title: New to BGA: Stem and Branch, How to Play on BGA

games.title: Dice Mission
games.opinion: Dice Mission is a Yahtzee-style luck game. The reviewer enjoyed the mechanic where failing to complete missions (busting) awards gadget cards, which help in solving more complex missions. They would play it if invited and in the mood.

summary: This video provides a detailed overview and review of the board game Dice Mission. It explains the core gameplay, different mission types (solo, versus, team, special), the two main phases (Mission and Debriefing), and the role of gadget cards. The reviewer also discusses the mobile experience and gives their personal rating for the game.
classification: new game including how to play and rating
_vid_title: New to BGA: Dice Mission, How to Play on BGA

games.title: Cartographers Heroes
games.opinion: This standalone expansion offers similar gameplay to the original Cartographers, but introduces new maps, new scoring and season cards, and replaces rune cards with hero cards. The hero mechanics allow players to destroy monsters, adding a new strategic layer. The game provides great setup options, allowing players to mix and match content from both the base game and the expansion. The reviewer slightly prefers the hero mechanics over the original ruins.

games.title: Cartographers
games.opinion: The base game that Cartographers Heroes expands upon. It shares the same core gameplay elements and is also highly rated by the reviewer, who enjoys playing it on BoardGameArena.

summary: This video reviews Cartographers Heroes, a standalone expansion for the board game Cartographers. It explains and demonstrates the new features introduced, including two new maps, hero cards that replace rune cards, new scoring and season cards, and unique ambush card abilities. The reviewer illustrates how hero tiles can defeat monsters on the map and highlights the extensive custom setup options available on BoardGameArena, allowing players to combine elements from both the base game and the expansion. The video concludes with a positive personal rating for both games.
classification: new game including how to play and rating
_vid_title: New to BGA: Cartographers Heroes — How to Play on BGA.

games.title: Good Fortune
games.opinion: The goal is to collect the most points by gathering charms of the same color or symbol. Points are scored at the end of the game by multiplying the pips on the dice symbol by the number of tiles collected for each matching set. Players take turns playing tiles onto the center board, matching colors/symbols. The player with the lowest or highest value tile (depending on the round's token) plays first. If a played tile doesn't match any existing colors/symbols, it's flipped over and no collection occurs for that turn.

summary: This video provides a detailed explanation of how to play the board game Good Fortune. It covers the game's objective, turn structure, tile placement rules, and end-game scoring mechanics.
classification: how to play
_vid_title: Good Fortune – How to Play Tutorial (Board Game Arena)

games.title: Reef Gardens
games.opinion: The video demonstrates the core mechanics of planting coral and surfacing divers to score points, explaining special tiles and rules for connecting coral. It highlights strategies for surrounding underwater stations and managing limited divers.

summary: This video provides a quick 'how to play' guide for the board game Reef Gardens, focusing on the short game version. It covers custom options, game setup, turn actions including planting corals from the nursery and surfacing divers to receive research funds and points. The explanation includes details on scoring for underwater stations based on adjacent coral, the function of sonar tiles and special shell tiles, rules for expanding coral reefs, and how to claim unclaimed coral. Final scoring conditions are also briefly outlined.
classification: how to play
_vid_title: Reef Gardens – How to Play Tutorial (Board Game Arena)

games.title: Ghosts Galore
games.opinion: The reviewer enjoys the drafting tiles, the many ways to score, and the push-your-luck element of getting tiles. They appreciate the simple gameplay of placing nine tiles, but noted that drafting can sometimes drag the turns.

summary: This video introduces 'Ghosts Galore,' a 2-5 player tile-placement game new to BoardGameArena (BGA). It provides a comprehensive 'how to play' guide, demonstrating the game's mechanics, tile types, and scoring rules on the BGA platform. The video concludes with a review, offering the reviewer's personal rating and thoughts on the game's strengths and weaknesses.
classification: new game including how to play and rating
_vid_title: New to BGA: Ghosts Galore — Is It Worth Your Time? How to Play On BGA

games.title: Ghost Galore
games.opinion: A tile placement game with a unique drafting mechanic and interesting scoring variety per monster type. The reviewer likes it and expects it to reach full release soon.

games.title: Peely Peely
games.opinion: A mostly standard trick-taking game that is spiced up each round by modifier cards that change the rules or goals, keeping gameplay fresh.

games.title: Carnuta
games.opinion: A fun hand management game about collecting resources and making potions, with satisfying combo potential through card synergies.

games.title: Day One
games.opinion: A collection tile placement game where players spread across a map, place huts, and complete objective cards. The reviewer found it fun and notable for its clever card-sliding mechanic.

games.title: Space Lab
games.opinion: The reviewer's favorite game of the month, rating it a 9. A card drafting and objective game with clever mechanics around card collection and color management. Plays very well on BGA.

summary: The Digital Board Gamer presents their top 5 favorite new beta or full releases on Board Game Arena from January 2026, covering Ghost Galore, Peely Peely, Carnuta, Day One, and Space Lab, with brief explanations of each game's mechanics and their personal impressions.
classification: new game including how to play and rating
_vid_title: Top 5 in 5 | January 2026 — Best New Games on Board Game Arena From Jan 2026.

games.title: Captain Flip: Isla Bomba
games.opinion: A great expansion for an already great game, bringing four new boards and five new treasure maps with unique effects.

summary: This video introduces and explains the 'Isla Bomba' expansion for the board game Captain Flip. It details the new content, including five new treasure maps with various effects like gaining coins, having bombs, or stealing from opponents. It also showcases four new adventure boards: The Submarine, The Prison, The Observatory, and Isla Bomba, explaining the unique scoring conditions and actions associated with each. The video concludes by demonstrating how to select these new expansion boards and maps on Board Game Arena.
classification: new game including how to play
_vid_title: How to Play Captain Flip Expansion — Isla Bomba

games.title: Postcards
games.opinion: Postcards is a 2-4 player hand management game where players travel across France on a bike to complete four postcards. Players play three travel cards per turn to take actions such as moving their bike to an adjacent region, drawing new postcards from a supply or discarding them, and placing camp tokens on campsites in regions to gain points or effects. Postcards require specific stamps and the player's bike to be in the indicated region to be sent, scoring points and allowing them to draw a gift card. The game ends when a player completes four postcards, followed by final scoring based on itinerary cards, unsent postcards, and gift cards.

summary: This video provides a detailed tutorial on how to play the board game Postcards (2025), covering the objective, player actions, mechanics like placing stamps and camp tokens, and the final scoring process.
classification: new game including how to play
_vid_title: How to Play Postcards on BGA

games.title: Space Lab
games.opinion: If you enjoy drafting and gaining cards, this game has a unique way of gaining cards. The goals and project cards are fun, and there are many ways to build your space lab. It's a perfect game on BGA for me; I love the simple card play, looking for combos, and trying to find and build the perfect lab.

summary: This video provides an introduction to Space Lab, detailing the game's objectives and rules through a 'How to Play' segment, followed by a comprehensive review where the reviewer gives the game a personal score of 9/10.
classification: new game including how to play and rating
_vid_title: New to BGA: Space Lab — Is It Worth Your Time?

games.title: Tembo: Survival on the Savanna
games.opinion: Tembo is a cooperative game with simple turns, offering many choices. It is a great solo experience, especially on BoardGameArena, which is recommended for initial plays. While there are various maps available, they tend to feel similar, leading to a 'samey' experience after multiple playthroughs. Communication in the digital turn-based format can be challenging as players might not actively review others' moves outside of their turn.

summary: This video provides a detailed 'how to play' guide and a review of the co-operative board game 'Tembo: Survival on the Savanna' on BoardGameArena. It covers the game's objective (moving the Matriarch elephant to the destination), winning and losing conditions (energy, cards, lions, elephants), main actions (building savanna, placing elephants, using support tokens), and card effects (Matriarch, lion). The reviewer also discusses the game's suitability for turn-based play and solo play, and shares a personal rating of 7/10, noting a potential for the game to feel repetitive over time.
classification: new game including how to play and rating
_vid_title: New to BGA: Tembo — Survival on the Savanna. How to play on BGA

games.title: 7 Wonders Dice
games.opinion: The game feels more random, offers fast games, and has a similar feel to other 7 Wonders games, though it has the same starts. The reviewer likes it but doesn't love it, considering it the weakest in the 7 Wonders series, but still enjoyable for quick plays.

summary: This video provides a 'How to Play' and review for the game '7 Wonders Dice,' a 2-7 player roll & write game that takes approximately 10 minutes. It explains the game's objective (most points), dice mechanics, resource management (warehouse), building construction costs, military aspects, and wonder building. The reviewer also discusses the game's suitability for turned-based play and offers his overall rating.
classification: new game including how to play and rating
_vid_title: New to BGA: 7 Wonders Dice — Is It Worth Your Time? How to Play & review on BGA

games.title: Tabletop Simulator
games.opinion: A sandbox-style system, not a rule-based game, where you control pieces on a digital table. The best feature is the workshop, where users create and share modules for various board games (e.g., Uno, Settlers of Catan, Nemesis). Be aware that modules are not programmed, so players need to know the game rules themselves. It was very useful during COVID for playing digitally with friends.

games.title: Root
games.opinion: A war game with a cute, cuddly theme but a deep strategic core. Each faction plays drastically different, making the digital app one of the best ways to learn the game. Players need to understand how each faction operates to play effectively.

games.title: Spirit Island
games.opinion: A very well-loved cooperative game where players defend an island from invaders. The reviewer enjoys playing co-op digital games because the AI is designed to replicate the physical game's automa, making it feel like playing against the game itself rather than a human opponent.

games.title: Gloomhaven
games.opinion: A lot of fun and great for solo play, as the game handles enemy mechanics and bookkeeping digitally. The co-op mode is currently broken, but the reviewer still highly recommends it.

games.title: Frosthaven
games.opinion: The younger brother of Gloomhaven, currently in Early Access. The reviewer played a bit in beta and noted a more significant town-building aspect compared to the original. The digital app is expected to greatly assist with managing these new features.

games.title: Dune: Imperium
games.award_category: 2022 Tabletop Game of the Year, 2022 Expert Game of the Year, 2022 Advanced Game of the Year, 2021 Best Sci-Fi Board Game, 2021 Expert Game of the Year, 2021 Best Card Game, 2021 Best Small Publisher Game, Tri Toc D'Or
games.opinion: A popular worker-placement and deck-building hybrid with a very interesting concept. It has a high player count on Steam and offers cool single-player content. Dire Wolf, the developer, does an awesome job with their apps.

games.title: Clank!
games.opinion: A dungeon-exploring deck-builder where players go down into a dungeon to find loot and escape before a dragon gets angry. The reviewer enjoys the physical game, but notes that the maps in the digital version feel a bit boring as they don't change. It does offer cool solo content and an expansion.

games.title: Set a Watch: Digital Edition
games.opinion: A new game about watching at night and defending a camp from attacks using dice and character abilities. There's also a camp phase for upgrading. The reviewer played the demo and found it pretty cool, planning a full review soon.

games.title: Cascadia
games.opinion: Another game from Dire Wolf, described as a great tile-placement puzzle game with cool challenges. No expansions are available yet for the digital version.

games.title: Small World
games.opinion: One of the earlier and classic digital board game apps. The reviewer played it extensively against the computer and appreciates the affordable additional content. It's considered a classic digital game.

games.title: Galaxy Trucker
games.opinion: An older game where players quickly build a spaceship from tiles under a timer, then embark on missions, deliver goods, and fend off pirates. It features a cool single-player campaign with a storyline and is highly recommended for those who enjoy quick-build games and chaotic gameplay.

games.title: Dominion
games.opinion: The base game is free to play, but expansions are very expensive (e.g., $131.99 for all 16). The reviewer has played many hours on the free version thanks to the 'Daily Challenges' that provide random card sets. The game plays insanely fast, and the hard AI is extremely difficult, winning 95% of the time. It's a cool app for quick games or trying the free version on Steam or iOS.

summary: The video provides an overview of the Steam Board Game Fest, highlighting various digital board games on sale or recently released. The reviewer, 'The Digital Board Gamer,' discusses several popular and classic titles, offering opinions on their digital adaptations, unique features like Tabletop Simulator's workshop, and the pricing of games and their expansions. The focus is on games not typically found on BoardGameArena but available on Steam for digital play, including cooperative, deck-building, worker placement, and puzzle genres.
classification: review
_vid_title: Beyond BGA: 12 Digital Board Games I Found at Steam's Board Game Fest

games.title: Pili Pili
games.opinion: It's an okay trick-taking and bidding game with some unique, sometimes crazy rules. The reviewer enjoyed a few games but doesn't see it as one their group will repeatedly play, possibly due to some rules bending or breaking the game. It plays well on mobile.

summary: This video provides an overview and review of the game Pili Pili, a 2-8 player bidding and trick-taking game that takes about 13 minutes. It includes a detailed explanation of how to play the game, highlighting its unique bidding rules and mission cards, followed by the reviewer's personal rating.
classification: new game including how to play and rating
_vid_title: New to BGA: Pili-Pili — Is It Worth Your Time?

games.title: Space Lab
games.opinion: The video provides a detailed explanation of how to play the game, covering setup, turn actions, card anatomy, and scoring rules.

summary: This video provides a comprehensive 'how to play' guide for the board game Space Lab (2025). It covers the game's objectives, player count, playing time, and then dives into detailed instructions on gameplay mechanics such as placing objective cards, drawing lab cards, taking cards from the recruitment area, playing lab cards by paying costs, understanding card features (cost, space agency, specialty, scoring), and acquiring project cards. It also explains how different cards and fulfilled goals contribute to the final score, and when the game ends.
classification: how to play
_vid_title: Space Lab: How to Play Tutorial on Board Game Arena

games.title: Ghosts Galore
games.opinion: Ghosts Galore is a 2-5 player tile placement game where players draft tiles to build a path connecting different types of doors and collect monsters for points. Players have a hidden tile to strategically place, can rotate tiles during placement, and can dead-end paths. Scoring is based on connecting bone doors (2 points per track section), tooth doors (2 points per track section), other paths (1 point per track section), and points associated with various monster types on the tiles.

summary: This video provides a quick run-through of the beta game Ghosts Galore. It explains the game's setup, including the concept of hidden tiles and drawing from a tile deck. The core gameplay loop of drafting and placing tiles to build paths is demonstrated, along with rules for rotation and valid placements. The video also details the scoring mechanisms, which involve connecting specific types of doors and collecting different monster symbols on the tiles.
classification: new game including how to play
_vid_title: Ghosts Galore: Easy How to Play Tutorial

games.title: Spirited
games.opinion: The reviewer enjoys the press-your-luck mechanic and manipulating the road. He notes that while busting can be frustrating, the game helps to mitigate this somewhat. He appreciates the multi-step, turn-based gameplay on BoardGameArena and recommends learning it solo. There are 12 alliances and 12 wonders, offering variety.

summary: This video provides an overview and review of the board game Spirited, which is new to BoardGameArena (BGA). It covers the game's objective, core mechanics like the 'Road' and 'Camps', how 'Wonders' and 'Allegiances' are formed, and the press-your-luck element with 'busting'. The reviewer demonstrates a typical turn on BGA, explains various actions and rules using a cheat sheet, and concludes with a personal score and recommendation.
classification: new game including how to play and rating
_vid_title: New to BGA: Spirited — Is It Worth Your Time?

games.title: 7 Wonders Dice
games.opinion: This video provides a detailed explanation of the rules, gameplay, and scoring mechanics for the game, including how to construct buildings, build wonders, manage resources, and utilize different dice types.

summary: This video is a comprehensive tutorial on how to play 7 Wonders Dice. It covers the game's objective, turn structure, the three main actions players can take (construct a building, build a wonder, or pass for gold), how resource costs and discounts work, military interactions, and the specific functions of each colored die and associated buildings. It concludes with an explanation of game-ending conditions.
classification: how to play
_vid_title: 7 Wonders Dice Tutorial - How to Play on Board Game Arena.

games.title: Carnuta
games.opinion: Carnuta is a hand management and collection game that reminds the reviewer of Splendor-style games due to its simple rules and strategic depth. The reviewer enjoys the scoring puzzle and hunting for specific card combinations. While there is some randomness, it is not a significant drawback for this quick and fun game. It plays great in a turn-based format with few turns and two actions per turn, and also performs well on mobile platforms. Overall, it's a solid game that the reviewer enjoyed in beta.

summary: This video provides an overview of the gameplay mechanics for Carnuta, including how to play and score, followed by a subjective review of the game, highlighting its appeal for fans of Splendor-style games and turn-based play, ultimately giving it a rating of 7/10.
classification: new game including how to play and rating
_vid_title: New to BGA: Carnuta — Is It Worth Your Time?

games.title: Up or Down
games.opinion: A fun card game where players build ascending or descending stacks, scoring based on size and color. Plays really well on BGA.

games.title: Miums
games.opinion: A Yahtzee-style dice game with only one roll per turn, but card powers and combos make it interesting and driven by strategy.

games.title: Guilds of the Merchant Explorers
games.opinion: A flip and write exploration game where players connect cities and spread out across a map each era. Played quite a bit in February and March.

games.title: Let's Go to Japan
games.opinion: A cool drafting game about planning a trip to Japan, matching activities to days of the week. Recommended for fans of drafting games.

games.title: Lord of the Rings: Fate of the Fellowship
games.opinion: A pandemic-style cooperative game with a Lord of the Rings theme. Played a ton of it especially solo, but it was removed from BGA in late 2025.

games.title: Pioneer Days
games.opinion: A dice resource management game with a clever disaster track mechanic. Got 72 plays in just a few months and even reached the top 10 in rankings.

games.title: Skull King
games.opinion: A fun and chaotic trick-taking game with a great bidding system and special pirate-themed cards. Played constantly with a group of friends.

games.title: Ariato
games.opinion: A drafting combo game where planning around round objectives is key. Enjoyed the drafting and strategic planning aspects quite a bit.

games.title: Welcome to the Moon
games.opinion: A flip and write game with eight different adventure maps that keep it fresh. Stands out from the other Welcome To games due to its variety, and is a great turn-based experience.

games.title: Stone Spine Architects
games.opinion: A dungeon-building drafting game with a market system and path-based scoring. The reviewer's top game of 2025, praising its depth and drafting mechanics.

summary: The Digital Board Gamer counts down their personal top 10 favorite Board Game Arena games that released in full in 2025, covering a variety of genres including card games, dice games, flip and write, drafting, trick-taking, and resource management games. The list includes brief explanations of each game's mechanics and why the reviewer enjoyed them.
classification: best of year
_vid_title: My Top 10 Board Game Arena Games of 2025

games.title: Dewan
games.opinion: The reviewer enjoys the exploration and race elements, along with the need for planning ahead. Despite losing multiple times, they still want to play it, finding it an enjoyable experience.

summary: This video provides an overview and review of the board game Dewan, newly available on BoardGameArena (BGA). It explains the game's mechanics, including scoring points by completing stories, collecting resources (berries, fire), placing camps to expand across the board (zones), and how terrain cards are used for movement and tucking for story completion. The reviewer also briefly mentions mobile playability and the potential for future scenarios.
classification: new game including how to play and rating
_vid_title: New to BGA: Dewan — Is It Worth Your Time?

games.title: Pili Pili
games.opinion: The reviewer explains the rules of this trick-taking game where the goal is to not collect six 'Pili Peppers'. The deck runs from 1 to 55 with one joker. Players bid on how many tricks they think they'll take, and if their bid is incorrect, they receive 'Pili Peppers'. The game features 36 mission cards that add unique twists each round.

summary: This video is a tutorial on how to play the trick-taking card game Pili Pili. The reviewer explains the game's objective, card values, bidding rules (emphasizing that the sum of bids cannot equal the total number of tricks to ensure a loser), and how 'Pili Peppers' are collected as penalties for incorrect bids. The video also mentions the variety of 36 mission cards that change gameplay each round.
classification: how to play
_vid_title: How to Play Pili-Pili on BGA (Quick Rules Overview)

games.title: Booty Dice
games.opinion: It is a push-your-luck dice game similar to King of Tokyo, focusing on eliminating opponents or gathering doubloons. The reviewer finds it okay, better with more players, and would play if invited but wouldn't initiate a game.

summary: This video provides an overview and review of the game Booty Dice, highlighting its rules, gameplay mechanics, and a personal rating from the reviewer. It also discusses how the game works on mobile devices and its suitability for different player counts.
classification: new game including how to play and rating
_vid_title: New to BGA: Booty Dice — Is It Worth Your Time?

games.title: Carnuta
games.opinion: Carnuta is a 2-4 player collection-style game where players perform two actions per turn from four possible options: taking cards, playing cards, taking runes, or flipping runes. The game involves managing a hand limit of three cards and utilizing runes to play cards, with bonus ingredients and rune slots unlocking as more cards are played. Scoring is based on collected ingredients and bonus items, with a wild 'salt' icon available. The game ends when a player plays their 11th card, followed by one final turn for all players.

summary: This video provides a detailed 'how to play' guide for the board game Carnuta, covering its setup, turn actions, special abilities like the 'sickle', game end conditions, and scoring mechanics. It demonstrates gameplay elements and explains how different card types contribute to the final score.
classification: new game including how to play
_vid_title: Carnuta Tutorial – How to Play on BGA

games.title: Ark Nova
games.opinion: Number one on BGA every single month of 2025. The most popular game on the platform, partly due to its long play time factoring into the popularity metric.

games.title: Terraforming Mars
games.opinion: Consistent number two on BGA throughout all of 2025 with very little movement.

games.title: Catan
games.opinion: Stayed in the top 10, trading positions with Wingspan a few times throughout the year.

games.title: Wingspan
games.opinion: Stayed in the top 10, trading positions with Catan a few times throughout the year.

games.title: Azul
games.opinion: Consistent top 10 game on BGA throughout 2025.

games.title: Forest Shuffle
games.opinion: Biggest gainer in the top 10. Started at 9 in January, peaked at 6 in September, and ended the year at 7.

games.title: Ticket to Ride Map Pack
games.opinion: Only two months of data. Started at 62 in November and jumped to 15 in December. Limited data makes it hard to assess trends.

games.title: Castle Combo
games.opinion: Clearly the biggest gainer in its group. Started at 40 in January and climbed all the way to 20 by December.

games.title: Harmonies
games.opinion: Made it into the top 10 at one point but ended just outside it at 11th place.

games.title: Sea Salt and Paper
games.opinion: Some fluctuation, ranging between 15 and 24 throughout the year.

games.title: Dead Cells
games.opinion: Most interesting game on the list. Came in low, fell off for a few months, came back with an update, then shot back up to 26 with the final update releasing all maps. Reviewer expects it to drop off again in a month or two.

games.title: White Castle
games.opinion: Was a hot game of the month, moving from 24 to 18, which was enough of a percentage change to qualify.

games.title: Flip 7
games.opinion: One of the biggest movers overall. Jumped from 67 to as low as 14, ending the year at 21. Definitely the game with the most movement in its group.

games.title: Concordia
games.opinion: Came in late in the year and dropped down the rankings. Reviewer is curious whether it can stay in the bottom 20 or 30.

games.title: Galactic Cruise
games.opinion: Came in late in the year and reached as low as 21 in the rankings.

games.title: Skull King
games.opinion: Started at 87 and worked its way all the way down to 33 by the end of the year.

games.title: Terramysa
games.opinion: Most fluctuating game in the 31-40 group. Started around 50s, went to 66, then came back to the lower 40s.

games.title: Dartmoor
games.opinion: Came in fast, dropped all the way to 28, but ended at 51.

games.title: Welcome To
games.opinion: Some movement between the 40s and upper 50s, ending in the low 50s. Mostly consistent.

games.title: Age of Innovation
games.opinion: Biggest faller in its group. Started at 26, then slowly declined to 50 and then 57 in the final months. Slowly losing popularity.

games.title: Challengers Beach Club
games.opinion: Started fast at 20 but shot back up to 52. Not a lot of data yet.

games.title: Gaia Project
games.opinion: Had a big spike up to 75 at one point but mostly consistent otherwise.

games.title: Zenith
games.opinion: Fell off the list for a short month but came back. Probably the biggest faller in its group.

games.title: Darwin's Journey
games.opinion: Started around 40, fell all the way to 89-88, then ended at 66. One of the bigger decliners.

games.title: Altered
games.opinion: Went as low as 25 at one point but ended near 75 at year's end. Significant fluctuation.

games.title: Quinto
games.opinion: Only three months of data. Started at 15, then around 50, then the 70s. Very limited data.

games.title: Soloist
games.opinion: Fell off the list and came back on later in the year.

games.title: Papio
games.opinion: Fell off the list twice and came back both times.

games.title: Railroad Ink
games.opinion: Fell off the list for one month. Possibly only ranked around 103-105 during that period.

games.title: Castles of Mad King Ludwig
games.opinion: Only four months of data. Started at 28 and went all the way up to the mid-to-high 90s. Biggest fall-off in its group.

games.title: Captain Flip
games.opinion: Has been on the list every month but has drifted toward the higher (worse) end of the rankings later in the year.

summary: The reviewer, the Digital Board Gamer, presents a year-in-review analysis of Board Game Arena's top 100 most popular games in 2025. Using monthly screenshots taken throughout the year, they track the ranking movements of games from January to December, highlighting the biggest gainers, fallers, and most consistent performers. The video uses graphs to visualize trends and discusses methodology, including how BGA calculates popularity using game count and play time.
classification: best of year
_vid_title: The Hottest and Not-So-Hot Games of 2025 on Board Game Arena

games.title: Agent Avenue
games.opinion: It features an 'I cut, you choose' mechanism which is quite a bit of fun and works really well. It's a very quick game, often finishing in 1-2 minutes on BGA. The advanced mode adds more complexity and makes it more interesting by introducing Black Market cards. It's a game the reviewer will be suggesting and playing with people.

summary: This video introduces Agent Avenue, a new 2-player bluffing race game on BoardGameArena (BGA). It provides an overview and review, explaining the basic gameplay mechanics, how cards affect movement, and different winning/losing conditions. It also briefly covers the advanced mode which introduces Black Market cards with special effects. The reviewer gives a personal rating and discusses whether the game is worth your time.
classification: new game including how to play and rating
_vid_title: New to BGA: Agent Avenue — Is It Worth Your Time?

games.title: Cry Baby
games.opinion: Cry Baby is a trick-taking game played in 4 rounds of 7 tricks each. Players choose card orientation, bet on tricks they think they can win, and earn points for successful bets. The player who plays the lowest card in a trick gets a 'cry baby' action, allowing them to flip a card for better value or gain a point.

summary: This video provides a comprehensive guide on how to play the trick-taking board game Cry Baby. It covers the game's goal, content, choosing card orientation, bidding with unique bet cards, following suit during tricks, and the special 'Cry Baby' action for the player who plays the lowest card in a trick, allowing them to either flip a card or gain a point. The video illustrates gameplay examples and scoring mechanics over four rounds.
classification: how to play
_vid_title: Cry Baby (Chouineurs) – How to Play This Clever Trick-Taking Game

games.title: Popcorn
games.opinion: The reviewer liked the movie theme and the deck/bag builder mechanics. However, they found the game to be a bit mean due to player interaction (stealing meeples) and considered it a little long for its complexity. Overall, it's an okay game to play if in the mood.

summary: This video provides an overview and review of the board game Popcorn, a 2-4 player deck/bag builder. The reviewer explains the game's objective, setup, phases of play (buying/advertising, showings, end of round), and scoring mechanisms. He highlights key mechanics such as adding meeples to bags, buying movies and theater seats, activating advertising tokens, and placing meeples to earn points and bonuses. The video concludes with a personal rating and final thoughts on the game's theme, mechanics, and player interaction.
classification: new game including how to play and rating
_vid_title: New to BGA: Popcorn — Is It Worth Your Time?

games.title: Stone Age
games.opinion: One of the older games on BGA, a cool worker placement with dice that helped it shine on the platform. Has been around long enough to accumulate plays and consistently appears in the top 50 hot list.

games.title: King Domino
games.opinion: A really fast and simple game done very well on BGA.

games.title: Catan
games.opinion: Doesn't need much introduction. Many people got into the hobby playing this game. A huge game that is almost always in the top 10, ranked around number three for most of the year.

games.title: Backgammon
games.opinion: A perfect example of a classic game that thrives online, likely due to turn-based play.

games.title: Coloretto
games.opinion: Mentioned as one of the original games on BGA at launch. Still available to play.

games.title: Can't Stop
games.opinion: A great, real simple game that gives you the 'just one more game' feeling. Has been on BGA since early 2011. Possibly more popular in Europe than the US.

games.title: Puerto Rico
games.opinion: Mentioned as one of the original games on BGA at launch. Still available to play.

games.title: Race for the Galaxy
games.opinion: One of the oldest games on the list, out since November 2010. Plays in just over 5 minutes, which combined with its longevity makes 16 million plays easy to understand. Has an app as well with more expansion content.

games.title: Cu Birds
games.opinion: A game the reviewer is not familiar with, but its very fast 6-minute play time is seen as a key driver of its high play count.

games.title: Wingspan
games.opinion: A game the reviewer has played a lot. Consistently in third or fourth position on the monthly top games list. Could thrive even more on BGA if expansions were added, but expansions are intentionally reserved for the paid app.

games.title: 6 Nimmt!
games.opinion: A game the reviewer hasn't played. Noted as playing fast with painful decisions and being quite mean.

games.title: Splendor
games.opinion: Its strength is how quickly you can get playing. Has more thinky-ness compared to some of the other quick games on the list.

games.title: Ark Nova
games.opinion: The most popular game on BGA overall. Impressive to make the list given it is a newer (2023) and longer game. Could move up a spot next year.

games.title: Seasons
games.opinion: A fun card drafting game with dice. Being 13 years old and always appearing on the tail end of the top games list (80-90 range), it is no surprise it made the all-time list.

games.title: Hanabi
games.opinion: A cooperative game about fireworks. The reviewer finds it slightly easier digitally due to better information management compared to the physical version.

games.title: Lucky Numbers
games.opinion: One of the fastest games on the list at 5 minutes average, which clearly drives its high play count.

games.title: 7 Wonders
games.opinion: A classic game that plays incredibly fast digitally compared to its physical counterpart, especially when everyone knows the rules. No surprise it has 11 million plays.

games.title: 7 Wonders Duel
games.opinion: One of the only games where the two-player version is more popular than the original. A great game to play on the platform and one the reviewer recommends checking out.

games.title: Carcassonne
games.opinion: Another gateway game most people have played. Having four expansions on BGA really helps its play count. Reviewer encourages checking out the expansions if you haven't.

games.title: Ticket to Ride
games.opinion: BGA removes setup and cleanup time, bringing the game down from 30-40 minutes to about 10 minutes, which the reviewer sees as a key reason it really shines on the platform.

games.title: Yahtzee
games.opinion: A mass market game that very few people haven't played physically. Its wide recognition and long time on the platform explain its high ranking.

games.title: Azul
games.opinion: The reviewer was a little surprised it topped the list, but considers it a great game — really strategic and fast. Works so well on BGA that there are three versions available. The original has over 24 million plays.

games.title: Terraforming Mars
games.opinion: Always the number two game on BGA in popularity but did not break the 5 million plays threshold for the top 20 list. The reviewer expects it will likely make the list eventually, especially as more expansions are added.

games.title: Altered
games.opinion: A TCG card game that was the fastest 2024 game to hit 1 million plays on BGA, currently sitting at 1.8 million.

games.title: Castle Combo
games.opinion: A notable 2024 game on BGA with 1.4 million plays, mentioned as one to watch for future lists.

games.title: Azul: Summer Pavilion
games.opinion: An Azul variant currently at just under 500,000 plays — nowhere near the numbers of the original Azul.

games.title: Dominion
games.opinion: Mentioned as one of the original games listed when BGA launched in 2011, but notably is no longer available on the platform.

games.title: Flowers
games.opinion: The last game to just barely qualify for the million-plays club, with just over 1 million plays. Came out April 21, 2024.

summary: The Digital Board Gamer presents the top 20 most-played games of all time on Board Game Arena (BGA), based on data collected on January 3, 2026. Out of 1,385 games on the platform, 76 have over 1 million plays. The video counts down from number 20 (Stone Age at 5.57 million plays) to number 1 (Azul at over 24 million plays), sharing each game's total play count, release date on BGA, and average game length. The host also briefly discusses BGA's history, methodology for data collection, notable games that just missed the list, and fast-growing newer titles from 2024.
classification: other
_vid_title: Top 20 Most Played Games of All Time on BGA.

games.title: Texas Hold'Em
games.opinion: The reviewer likes poker and played it when younger, but it's not something they will play much on BoardGameArena. They think tournaments might be more engaging. Turn-based play is an option but might be slow.

summary: This video provides an overview and review of Texas Hold'Em on BoardGameArena. It covers the basic rules of the game, including how cards are dealt, betting rounds (small blind, big blind), the flop, turn, and river, and how hands are compared. The reviewer concludes by giving the game a score and their personal opinion on playing it on BGA.
classification: new game including how to play and rating
_vid_title: New to BGA: Texas Hold’em — Is It Worth Your Time?

games.title: Formula D
games.opinion: Formula D is a racing game with dice rolling and custom dice. The reviewer enjoys the racing aspect, gear shifting mechanics, and the strategic planning required for turns. The game also offers an advanced mode with various options like weather, tire selection, qualifying laps, custom construction, nitro fuel, and team play, as well as a solo version. However, the reviewer ultimately prefers 'Heat' over Formula D, finding Heat's card play and heat system more fun and engaging, while Formula D's dice rolling can feel a bit slow and predictable.

games.title: Heat
games.opinion: The reviewer prefers 'Heat' over 'Formula D', stating that they would go back to play Heat more. They like Heat's card play and heat system, finding it more fun and engaging. They also feel that Heat offers more dynamic gameplay compared to Formula D's somewhat predictable dice rolls.

summary: This video introduces Formula D as a new game on BoardGameArena, providing a detailed 'How to Play' guide for its basic rules, covering starting order, gear shifting, movement, cornering, wear points, collisions, and pit stops. It also highlights the extensive advanced options and multiple maps available. The video concludes with a subjective review, comparing it to 'Heat' and giving Formula D a score of 7/10.
classification: new game including how to play and rating
_vid_title: New to BGA: Formula D — Is It Worth Your Time?

games.title: Junk Drawer
games.opinion: Junk Drawer is a solid tile placement puzzle game with multiple goals that encourages planning ahead. It is quick and simple, making it a great fit for BoardGameArena, and offers a solo mode. The reviewer enjoys polyomino games and finds this one brings unique twists.

summary: This video provides a 'How to Play' guide and review for the game 'Junk Drawer', a polyomino puzzle game available on BoardGameArena. It explains the game's rules, including placing junk pieces into four different 5x5 drawers, completing various scoring goals, and demonstrating gameplay mechanics like rotating and flipping pieces. The video concludes with a review, praising the game's quick playtime, strategic depth with diverse goals, and solo play option, giving it a score of 7/10.
classification: new game including how to play and rating
_vid_title: New to BGA: Junk Drawer — Is It Worth Your Time?

games.title: Reforest
games.opinion: A solid hand management combo game that is well worth the reviewer's time. Enjoys the combo-hunting aspect, appreciates the clever minimalist design of using just one deck of cards, and plans to continue playing it on BGA.

games.title: Wingspan
games.opinion: A game the reviewer really enjoys and finds comparable to Reforest in terms of artwork and how abilities activate. Wishes the expansions were available on BGA.

games.title: Forest Shuffle
games.opinion: The reviewer finds it okay but does not enjoy it as much as Wingspan. Notes similarities to Reforest in terms of the card-paying cost mechanic.

summary: The reviewer introduces Reforest, a newly available hand management card game on Board Game Arena, explains how to play it including card placement, costs, shade tolerance, adjacency, stored energy, and scoring, then gives it a personal rating of 8 out of 10, recommending it especially to fans of Wingspan and Forest Shuffle.
classification: new game including how to play and rating
_vid_title: New to BGA: Reforest — Is It Worth Your Time?

games.title: Azure
games.opinion: Reviewer enjoys Azure more than most abstract strategy games, appreciating its simple rules with depth, variable setup via randomly oriented mountain tiles, beast tug-of-war mechanics, and good puzzle gameplay. Rates it a 7 out of 10, slightly above the usual 6 given to abstract strategy games.

summary: The Digital Board Gamer introduces Azure, a two-player abstract strategy area majority game on Board Game Arena. The video covers the rules including placing common stones, bond discounts, and the four beast mechanics (White Tiger, Blue Dragon, Black Tortoise, and Bird), then gives a personal rating of 7/10, recommending it for fans of simple but deep abstract strategy games with variable gameplay and turn-based play.
classification: new game including how to play and rating
_vid_title: New to BGA: Azure — Is It Worth Your Time?

games.title: Choconnect
games.opinion: The reviewer finds the game too slow to build up, with too much luck involved in drawing pieces. They appreciate the connect-style gameplay but dislike the long setup phase and randomness that can prevent strategic wins. Not a game they want to return to.

summary: The reviewer introduces Choconnect on Board Game Arena, an abstract strategy sliding game where players connect pieces of dark chocolate (3 in a row), milk chocolate (4 in a row), or white chocolate (5 in a row). They explain the mechanics, including how pieces slide when added to the board, and ultimately give the game a 4 out of 10, citing the overly long buildup phase and luck-of-the-draw mechanics as major drawbacks.
classification: new game including how to play and rating
_vid_title: New to BGA: Choconnect — Is It Worth Your Time?

games.title: Concordia
games.opinion: A solid medium-weight game with enjoyable deck building, action retrieval mechanics, and strategic card planning. The reviewer loved it in beta and plans to keep playing it on BGA regularly.

summary: The Digital Board Gamer provides a tutorial and review of Concordia on Board Game Arena (BGA), covering the rules, card actions, resource management, and building mechanics. The video walks through how to play on the two-player map, explains each card type, and concludes with the reviewer's personal rating of 8/10, recommending it especially for fans of medium-weight deck-building games.
classification: new game including how to play and rating
_vid_title: New to BGA: Concordia (Ranked top 30 on BGG)— Is It Worth Your Time?

games.title: Iye
games.opinion: A deep abstract strategy game with multiple ways to win, enjoyable scoring mechanics that double as movement options, but falls in the middle of the road like most abstract strategy games for the reviewer.

summary: The reviewer introduces Iye, a two-player abstract strategy game on BGA, explaining the rules including grid movement, majority scoring by tile color, special tile movements, and two win conditions (points or opponent unable to move). They demonstrate gameplay and give it a score of 6 out of 10.
classification: new game including how to play and rating
_vid_title: New to BGA: Iye — Is It Worth Your Time?

games.title: Miams
games.opinion: The reviewer really enjoyed this game, appreciating the uniqueness of having only one dice roll instead of multiple rerolls, the card and combo system that adds strategic depth, and the blend of dice and card mechanics. While acknowledging high randomness and that strong combos are not always guaranteed, they found the gameplay engaging enough to have played it extensively in beta and plan to continue playing.

summary: The reviewer introduces Miams, a two-to-five player dice and card game on Board Game Arena, providing a how-to-play tutorial covering dice rolling, skewer scoring, heart-crossing mechanics, card types, and end-game scoring. They then review the game, giving it an 8 out of 10, highlighting its unique single-roll mechanic, card combos, and overall enjoyable gameplay despite its inherent randomness.
classification: new game including how to play and rating
_vid_title: New to BGA: Miams — Is It Worth Your Time?

games.title: Diced Veggies
games.opinion: A fun, fast-playing dice contract game with an interesting puzzle element in the dice-cutting mechanic, though the digital version loses some charm without the physical cleaver. The reviewer enjoys playing it but wouldn't actively invite others to a game, though would happily accept an invitation.

summary: The reviewer introduces Diced Veggies, a newly released game on BGA, explaining how it works including the cut phase, cook phase, chef tokens, hype cards, and restocking mechanics. After walking through gameplay, the reviewer rates it a 6 out of 10, praising its fast turns and turn-based playability while noting the digital version lacks some of the physical charm.
classification: new game including how to play and rating
_vid_title: New to BGA: Diced Veggies — Is It Worth Your Time?

games.title: Cathood
games.opinion: Enjoyed the mechanic of unlocking extra action pawns, appreciates the modular board, pickup and delivery theme, and plans to return to it. Recommends planning early turns to gain extra actions quickly.

summary: The Digital Board Gamer provides a tutorial and review of Cathood on Board Game Arena, explaining the rules including movement, resource collection, building placement, pet shop actions, park statues, and special board spaces, before giving the game a score of 7 out of 10.
classification: new game including how to play and rating
_vid_title: New to BGA: Cathood — Is It Worth Your Time?

games.title: Wandering Towers
games.opinion: Loves the BGA adaptation's animations and sound effects. Enjoys the solo variant despite its difficulty. Finds it enjoyable at any player count up to six.

games.title: Welcome to the Moon
games.opinion: Favorite of the Welcome to trilogy. Enjoys the flip-and-write gameplay, solo variant, and campaign mode with eight missions.

games.title: Let's Go to Japan
games.opinion: Loves the Japanese theme and itinerary-building gameplay. Was an instant hit when played with her husband. Appreciates that real locations are used.

games.title: Pina Colada Dice
games.opinion: Describes it as silly fun, perfect for when too tired for a big game. Favorite quick/small game of 2025. Has played it an embarrassing number of times.

games.title: Flip Tunes
games.opinion: Loves the '90s cartoon-style artwork and nostalgia factor. Enjoys the deck-building gameplay and particularly favors the rabbit tune card.

games.title: Hutan
games.opinion: Finds it relaxing and therapeutic, similar to My Shelfie. Easy to learn but with depth. Has a special affection for the Pangolin token.

games.title: Quetzal
games.opinion: Considers it a hidden gem and now loves it more than Azul. Owns the physical copy and feels she has cracked the game. Praises the flawless BGA adaptation.

games.title: Diced Veggies
games.opinion: Was drawn to it during beta and loves the theme of cooking global recipes. Currently playing a turn-based game with a fellow board game YouTuber.

games.title: Cities
games.opinion: Calls it her favorite Phil Walker-Harding game. Loves designing neighborhoods with multiple ways to score points and finds it very balanced and fun.

games.title: The White Castle
games.opinion: Describes it as a masterpiece and the heaviest game on the list. Loves the worker placement mechanics, chaining combos, and the satisfaction of planning well within just nine turns.

summary: Sasha counts down her personal top 10 favorite board games released on Board Game Arena in 2025, out of 106 games she played that year. She covers each game's theme, mechanics, and what makes it enjoyable, ranging from quick casual games like Pina Colada Dice to the heavy worker placement of The White Castle, which takes her number one spot.
classification: best of year
_vid_title: Top 10 Board Games of 2025 | Board Game Arena Edition!

games.title: Cosmic Encounter
games.opinion: A chaotic and entertaining game where alien powers, flare cards, and artifact cards create unpredictable moments. The reviewer shares a funny story about losing a round after their power was swapped by an opponent's card.

games.title: Black Angel
games.opinion: Visually stunning with bright neon colors. A strategic dice and tile placement game with a cool sci-fi setting about robots guiding humanity's DNA to a new planet.

games.title: Andromeda's Edge
games.opinion: A spiritual successor to Dwellings of Elderale that the reviewer loves, having only played it digitally so far. A wonderful strategic game combining area influence, dice rolling, hand management, and worker placement.

games.title: Space Base
games.opinion: Loves the mechanism where everyone benefits from one player's dice roll, but finds the bookkeeping tedious and prefers playing it on Board Game Arena.

games.title: Welcome to the Moon
games.opinion: The reviewer's favorite in the Welcome to series. A flip-and-write game best enjoyed on Board Game Arena, with a fun campaign mode across eight adventures.

games.title: The Crew: The Quest for Planet 9
games.opinion: One of the first trick-taking games the reviewer enjoyed. A cooperative game with 50 missions, but notes it can be a hit or miss depending on the group.

games.title: Eternium
games.opinion: Considers it a hidden gem. A deck-building time travel game with gorgeous art and two expansions. Has played it multiple times on Board Game Arena and loves it.

games.title: Terraforming Mars
games.opinion: Took a year to get to the table and initially disliked it. Criticizes the poor production quality and player boards but recommends dual-layer player boards to improve the experience. Acknowledges the game's strengths despite its flaws.

games.title: Arcs
games.opinion: Initially bought out of FOMO but was blown away on first play. An innovative game that forces players out of their comfort zone with a unique trick-taking action selection system. One of the most innovative games the reviewer has played recently.

games.title: Nemesis
games.opinion: Considers it the best board gaming experience ever. A semi-cooperative alien survival game that feels like being in a movie. Creates unforgettable stories and keeps players paranoid throughout. Strongly recommends it to everyone.

games.title: Pioneer
games.opinion: An honorable mention TTRPG set in the near future of solar system exploration. The reviewer was drawn in by its authenticity and realism, written by a software architect with NASA experience. Currently on Kickstarter.

summary: The reviewer counts down their personal top 10 space-themed board games of all time, covering games ranging from cooperative survival experiences to strategic engine builders. Games discussed include Cosmic Encounter at #10 and Nemesis at #1, with an honorable mention for the TTRPG Pioneer currently on Kickstarter. The reviewer shares personal anecdotes, gameplay mechanics, and opinions on each game throughout the video.
classification: other
_vid_title: My Top 10 Space Themed Board Games!

games.title: Gazebo
games.opinion: A botanical garden domino game with area majority and territory building mechanics, kicking off Bitewing Games' travel line.

games.title: Holiday Hills
games.opinion: A collaborative winter-themed game with a fun double-sided board offering both nice and naughty modes for a spicy holiday season.

games.title: Garden Lake
games.opinion: A relaxing and rewarding pentomino tile-laying game designed by Uwe Rosenberg where players build garden lakes with water lilies and koi fish.

games.title: Cozy Stickerville
games.opinion: A cooperative city-building game with over 800 stickers featuring villages, farms, and activities like fishing and exploring mines.

games.title: A Place for All My Books
games.opinion: A puzzly book-gathering and organizing game where players arrange stacks of books in apartment rooms, featuring a unique solo mode.

games.title: Knitting Circle
games.opinion: A delightfully cozy tile game where players knit garments and collect buttons with feline friends, with charming yarn-collecting mechanics.

games.title: I Made You a Mixtape
games.opinion: A creative I-cut-you-choose card game about crafting the best mixtape from an album collection, judged on theme, flow, and balance.

games.title: Adulting
games.opinion: A relatable semi-cooperative deck-building game about balancing chores and self-care as a stressed adult, with a theme the reviewer personally connects with.

games.title: Tactim
games.opinion: A fast two-player auto-battler deck-building game inspired by classic arcade fighting games, also available on Board Game Arena.

games.title: Grunt Work
games.opinion: A hilarious party game where players make and interpret expressive grunting noises, simple and fun for larger groups.

games.title: Squirrel Away
games.opinion: A lively party game mixing dexterity, bluffing, and set collection as players sneakily grab acorn gems and claim how many they took.

games.title: Boba Kiki
games.opinion: A fascinating cooperative communication game based on the real psychological Boba-Kiki effect, connecting sounds and shapes across cultures.

games.title: Oh No, We Crashed
games.opinion: A fast cooperative pattern recognition game where players fix a damaged spaceship before oxygen runs out, great for quick play.

games.title: Twinkle
games.opinion: A tile-laying space exploration game featuring clear plastic tiles and a fun optional app that magically tallies up your score.

games.title: Brink
games.opinion: A deep worker placement, trading, and hidden voting game set in the Moonbreakers universe with heavy player interaction.

games.title: Forestry
games.opinion: A strategic euro game with dual worker placement focused on sustainability choices and ensuring a forest thrives while meeting demand.

games.title: Solar Gardens
games.opinion: A city-building game about carbon-neutral rooftop gardens that the reviewer loves thematically, featuring solar farms and windmill systems.

games.title: Aspen
games.opinion: A two-player game about growing the largest forest on a shared board by harnessing wind and sun while balancing growth with expansion.

games.title: Removal
games.opinion: A humorous open drafting and grid coverage game where players fill a removal truck with junk without breaking anything.

games.title: Tootin and Hollering
games.opinion: An absurdly funny fart-themed party game about competing in fart contests after eating calico beans, which the reviewer thinks their group will love.

games.title: Soda Jerk
games.opinion: A quick humorous card game about serving beverages while being a jerk to other players.

games.title: Tearable Quest
games.opinion: A wild real-time puzzle game where up to 50 players tear pages to match icons under a 2-minute timer, fighting bosses and looting treasure.

games.title: Ministry of Lost Things
games.opinion: A deep cooperative deduction game played through four sequential envelopes of puzzles, taking around 3 hours to complete.

games.title: Kokeshi
games.opinion: An engine-building game with innovative track-building mechanics set in the world of Japanese artisan doll crafting, demo-only at PAX and also on BGA.

games.title: Twisted Crumpets
games.opinion: A whimsical tile-laying game about crafting the most fantastical crumpet in the kingdom with special bonus actions.

games.title: Wispwood
games.opinion: A magical forest grid-coverage game where players place glowing wisp tiles to lure a curious cat while satisfying the wishes of wisps and trees.

games.title: Covenant
games.opinion: A set collection fantasy game about leading a dwarf clan to reclaim a sacred mountain, accumulating glory to become Monarch.

games.title: Graft
games.opinion: A push-your-luck open drafting game where players race to collect sets of goods and chain combos of cards for high scores.

games.title: Fromio
games.opinion: A standalone expansion to Fromage about Italian cheesemaking with simultaneous worker placement, offering more variety and paths to victory.

games.title: Tent
games.opinion: A cozy flip-and-write farming game set on an alien world where pioneers farm, explore, and fish while building up their homestead.

summary: Sasha reviewed her top 3 board games across 10 categories (abstract strategy, cozy, card games, party, space themed, environmental, humor, puzzle, fantasy, and farming) from the 434 games on the PAX Unplugged preview list, providing brief descriptions and highlights for each game ahead of the convention.
classification: other
_vid_title: PAX Unplugged Top 3 Games | Quick Preview

games.title: Splendor: Cities
games.opinion: Changes the end game condition by replacing noble tiles with city tiles requiring both prestige points and specific colored cards. The reviewer describes it neutrally without strong positive or negative sentiment.

games.title: Splendor: Trading Posts
games.opinion: The reviewer's favorite expansion. Grants ongoing abilities and powers that felt very rewarding, especially the gem-taking bonuses. Would choose this over all other expansions.

games.title: Splendor: The Orient
games.opinion: Adds additional cards with special one-time abilities. The reviewer explicitly states they do not like this expansion.

games.title: Splendor: Strongholds
games.opinion: Adds stronghold tokens to block or contest cards. The reviewer found it frustrating as players kept removing each other's strongholds, making the benefits hard to use. Not a fan of this expansion.

summary: The reviewer plays and evaluates all four Splendor expansion modules available on Board Game Arena — Cities, Trading Posts, Orient, and Strongholds — explaining how each one changes the base game and sharing personal opinions on each, ultimately recommending the Trading Posts expansion as their favorite.
classification: review
_vid_title: All Splendor Expansions Reviewed on BGA

games.title: Hey, That's My Fish!
games.opinion: Simple abstract strategy game that works best as a family game. Rules are very straightforward, but there is some planning involved with multi-step turns. Rated a 6, which is where the reviewer places most abstract strategy games.

summary: The reviewer introduces Hey, That's My Fish! on Board Game Arena, explaining the rules, gameplay, and mechanics of this 2-4 player abstract strategy game. Players move penguins across an ice tile board, collecting tiles and fish as they go, with the goal of collecting the most fish. The reviewer rates it a 6 out of 10, recommending it primarily as a family game.
classification: new game including how to play and rating
_vid_title: New to BGA: Hey, That’s My Fish! — Is It Worth Your Time?

games.title: YINSH
games.opinion: A classic and well-loved abstract strategy game with a clever blocking mechanism and a satisfying catch-up mechanic, but abstract strategy games are not the reviewer's preferred genre, landing it at a 6 out of 10.

summary: The reviewer introduces YINSH, a two-player abstract strategy game available on BGA, explaining its rules and mechanics including ring placement, marker flipping, and scoring by forming five in a row. They highlight the blocking mechanism and catch-up mechanic as standout features, note it works fine on mobile, and give it a 6/10 based on BGG's willingness-to-play scale, acknowledging its quality while admitting abstract strategy games are not their personal favorite.
classification: new game including how to play and rating
_vid_title: New to BGA: YINSH — Is It Worth Your Time?

games.title: Postcards
games.opinion: The reviewer finds it a solid family-weight game with simple actions and some action chaining via the camp mechanic, but feels it is a little too light for their personal taste.

summary: The Digital Board Gamer covers Postcards on Board Game Arena, providing a how-to-play tutorial explaining movement, stamping, camp actions, sending postcards, and scoring, followed by a brief review giving the game a 6 out of 10 on their willingness-to-play scale.
classification: new game including how to play and rating
_vid_title: New to BGA: Postcards — Is It Worth Your Time?

games.title: Waypoints
games.opinion: A simultaneous roll-and-write game with interesting variability from dice rolls. Reviewer enjoyed it at Analog and thinks it would work well on BGA due to its simultaneous turn structure.

games.title: Glory to Rome
games.opinion: A beloved 20-year-old multiple-use card game that is now very expensive and hard to find. Reviewer has held onto their copy for years and thinks BGA would be a great way to make it accessible again.

games.title: Lord of the Rings: Duel for Middle Earth
games.opinion: An abstracted two-player Lord of the Rings game with mechanisms that build on Seven Wonders Duel. Reviewer finds the military board and Frodo/Sam escape track thematically compelling and sees strong strategic depth.

games.title: Aiya
games.opinion: A mancala-style game the reviewer has only played three times. Admits to being selfish about wanting it on BGA mainly to play it more often and master it.

games.title: Wondrous Creatures
games.opinion: A tableau-building worker placement game with a large card deck, compared to Ark Nova and Wingspan. Reviewer believes BGA's async format would alleviate complaints about its long play time. Notably, this game was announced as having joined BGA in alpha after the video was recorded.

games.title: Scout
games.opinion: A light ladder-climbing card game with a clever double-sided hand mechanic. Reviewer is a fan of trick-taking games and thinks Scout fits perfectly as a quick, fun BGA title.

games.title: Civolution
games.opinion: A Stefan Feld civilization-themed dice placement game. Reviewer loves the many strategic paths and thinks BGA's automation of procedural phases would make it significantly more streamlined.

games.title: Undaunted: Normandy
games.opinion: A two-player WWII deck-building game with map control. Reviewer enjoys it as a lighter war game and sees potential for BGA to use it as a hook to sell the rest of the Undaunted series.

games.title: La Havre
games.opinion: Reviewer's top pick as a Uwe Rosenberg fan. Believes it would be mechanically straightforward to implement on BGA, has historically been one of the most requested games on the platform, and would expose new gamers to a classic.

games.title: War of the Ring
games.opinion: A heavier two-player Lord of the Rings strategy game with dice and cards. Reviewer has loved it for years, owns the deluxe edition, and participates in online tournaments. Considers it the most thematic Lord of the Rings experience available.

summary: Digital Board Gamer and Board Gaming Doctor (Phil) each share their personal top 5 games they wish were available on Board Game Arena. The video covers 10 games total (5 picks each), discussing why each would be a good fit for the platform in terms of mechanics, accessibility, and audience. A post-recording editor's note reveals that Wondrous Creatures was added to BGA in alpha shortly after the video was made.
classification: other
_vid_title: These 5 Games NEED to Come to BGA (Top Picks w/ Board Gaming Doctor)
